description_long: This sculpture features a relief panel depicting two human figures in classical attire, positioned on either side of a circular emblem or medallion. The left figure appears to be a partially draped woman holding a cloth or drapery, while the right figure is a man in period clothing, standing with his hands clasped in front. The background is a flat stone surface with subtle carved details, including the central medallion and faint architectural elements. The figures are rendered in a somewhat stylized, blocky manner, with visible segmentation and some missing or eroded parts, suggesting age or damage. The surface texture is rough and matte, with a predominantly gray coloration and some lighter highlights. The overall composition suggests a commemorative or historical theme, possibly referencing a specific event or era.
description: A stone relief sculpture showing two human figures flanking a central medallion, with rough, matte gray surfaces and classical attire. The figures are stylized and partially eroded, set against a flat carved background.
description_view_0: Front view showing both figures in full, with the central medallion and most surface details visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the sculpture's thin, relief-like profile and the depth of the carved figures.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the flat background with the relief figures and medallion in low detail.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again highlighting the thinness and relief nature of the sculpture.
attribute_subject_matter: two human figures in classical attire with a central medallion
attribute_location_context: The sculpture appears to be set against a stone or concrete wall, suggesting an outdoor urban or commemorative setting.
